page: Microsoft Azure EC8as_cc_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64, utilizing a cache structure that includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25% at 2894.58. Multi-core performance and memory read bandwidths generally fall within the average range, with a cached read-only small block memory bandwidth of 517.06 GB/sec and an uncached large block read bandwidth of 39.58 GB/sec. However, uncached large block write memory bandwidth is a relative bottleneck, registering in the bottom 25% at 22.39 GB/sec. LLM inference benchmarks show average performance for small 135M models but drop to weak levels for medium 7B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a dense memory allocation relative to its vCPU count, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute or local NVMe storage. Given the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and average database operation benchmarks, this server is well-suited for relational databases, caching layers like Redis, software compilation, and general-purpose memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure EC8as_cc_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 8.0 GB of memory per core. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server lacks local storage and integrated GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmark data highlights strong single-core CPU performance, alongside average multi-core execution, software compilation, and memory read bandwidth. Conversely, uncached large-block write memory bandwidth and medium-model LLM inference represent performance trade-offs, landing in the lower percentiles. Qualitatively, the instance provides a balanced, memory-dense profile without the added cost of local NVMe drives. It is best suited for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, Redis caching, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure EC8as_cc_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and strong single-core performance, designed for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure EC8as_cc_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized VM with 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and strong single-core CPU performance.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and strong single-core performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB RAM.
High memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU suitable for memory-bound tasks.
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks.
No bundled local storage, but compatible with Premium Storage.
Optimal for database operations, Redis caching, and software compilation.
